exercises.name: Suspension Body Saw From Elbow
exercises.alternateName: TRX Body Core Saw From Elbow
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, maintain neutral spine by setting your abdominals and keeping your glutes active.  

2.When you press forward, that’s when it’ll be most challenging so try to keep the distance between the bottom of your ribs and top of your hips set.

3.Let your feet be suspended to the straps. Use your elbows to support the body. Then raise your body off the ground.

4. Slowly swing your body forward, then backwards while keeping your elbows steady on the ground.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Body Saw on Hand
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, maintain a tight core. This will be most challenging when your arms are in front of your body and above your head.  

2.Let your feet be suspended to the straps. And arms stretched to the ground to support the body. Then raise your body off the ground.

3. Slowly swing your body forward, then backwards while keeping your arms and legs straight.

4.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Body Saw with Crunch from Elbow
exercises.alternateName: TRX Body Saw with Crunch from Elbow
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, maintain a neutral spine. 

2.When performing the crunch component, shorten the distance between your ribs and the top of your hips. 

3.Let your feet be suspended to the straps. Use your elbows to support the body. 

4.Raise your hips as high as you can so that it creates an inverted V-shape while keeping your elbows flat on the ground. Then slowly swing your body backwards.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Body Saw with Crunch from Hand
exercises.alternateName: TRX Body Saw With Crunch From Hand
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, maintain neutral spine. 

2.When performing the crunch aspect of the movement, shorten the distance between the bottom of your ribs and the top of your hips. 

3.Let your feet be suspended to the straps. Use your hands to support the body. 

4.Raise your hips as high as you can so that it creates an inverted V-shape while keeping your arms straight to the ground. Then slowly swing your body backwards.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Chest & Torso Stretch
exercises.description:
1.For the stretch, any number of muscle groups may be stretched. However, the main focus is your lats and your chest.

2.In order to isolate the lat, you’ll wanna fully extend your arm and lean forward from this position.

3.With your back at the equipment, grab the straps with your one arm stretched upwards, one foot to the front, while leaning your body forward. The other arm helps support the body while staying steady on the side, elbows bent.

4.Hold for approximately 20 – 30 seconds. When you’re done, repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Suspension Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1.When performing exercise, keep your abdominals engaged and glutes on. This will maintain a straight body throughout the exercise.

2.When you’re performing the fly, keep a relatively straight arm. A little bit of an elbow bend is okay. 

3.Squeeze from your chest to get back to the top of the movement.

4.With your back at the equipment, grab the straps with your arms in front of your body and heels up. This will be your starting position.

5.Slowly descend your body while spreading both arms to the side creating a straight line from one arm to the other. Then pull yourself back to starting position.

6.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

7.To make this exercise easier, move the straps shorter and have your body in a bit more of an upright position. To make it more challenging, lengthen the straps and move your feet towards the anchor point.

exercises.name: Suspension Chest Fly From Knee
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, maintain tight abs and set your glutes. This will help keep neutral spine and a straight line from your knees to your head. 

2.You’ll also wanna keep mostly a straight arm locked out at the elbow and make sure that your elbows and hands are about mid chest height.

3.With your back at the equipment, grab the straps with your arms in front of your body and your knees to the ground. This will be your starting position. 

4.Slowly descend your body while spreading both arms to the side creating a straight line from one arm to the other. Then pull yourself back to starting position. 

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions. 

6.To make this exercise more challenging, try the Standing Chest Fly. To make it easier, shorten the straps.

exercises.name: Suspension Chest Press
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you wanna keep your abs and your glutes engaged maintaining a straight line throughout your body.

2.At the bottom of the press, keep your elbows and hands roughly in line with mid chest at an about 90 degrees depth.

3.With your back at the equipment, grab the straps with your palms facing the ground and heels up. This will be your starting position.

4.Slowly descend your body keeping your elbows, hands in line with mid chest. Then pull yourself back to starting position.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

6.To make this exercise more challenging, lengthen the straps. To make it a little bit easier, shorten them.

exercises.name: Suspension Chest Press From Knee
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your abdominals engaged and glutes on. This will help maintain neutral spine and a straight line from your knees to your head.

2.At the bottom of the press, you’ll want your elbows at 90 degrees. Furthermore, you’ll want your elbows and hand in line roughly mid chest.

3. Begin positions with both knees to the ground. With your back at the equipment, grab the straps and slowly lean forward moving your arms to the side, elbows making 90 degrees.

4. To make this exercise more challenging, try the Standing Chest Press. To make it more easier, shorten the straps.

exercises.name: Suspension Chest Stretch
exercises.description:
1.When performing the stretch, you’ll be stretching your pec muscle and your interior delt. Ensure to keep a tall thoracic spine or open rib cage as you slowly lean forward and increase your stretch. 

2.With your back at the equipment, grab the straps with your arms spread to the sides. One foot in front of the other.   

3.Slowly lean forward and increase your stretch. Hold for approximately 20 – 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Suspension Cross Mountain Climber
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your abdominals engaged. You’ll cross your knee from one side to the opposite elbow on the other. 

2.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into starting position by balancing your weight on your hands, arms and elbows straight. 

3.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor.

4.Start with one leg, cross one knee to the opposite elbow. Then back to starting position. Proceed with the other knee.

exercises.name: Suspension Crossing Balance Lunge
exercises.alternateName: TRX Crossing Balance Lunge cross over
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure that you cross out to the side with your free leg rather than reaching behind you. 

2.Furthermore, you want to stand tall by driving your hips or getting your glutes active to create a straight line with your body.

3. Grab the handles for support. Slightly fall your body backwards, then raise one leg with your knee bent. This will be your starting position.

4.Slowly cross out to the side with your free leg as far as you can.  Then back to starting position.

5.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

6.Once you’ve completed one side, then do the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Crunch From Forearm
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure that your abdominals are engaged especially in the lengthen position. 

2.In the crunch position, shorten the distance between the bottom of your ribs and the top of your hips.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a plank position by balancing your weight on your elbows. 

4.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor. This will be your starting position.

5.Pull your legs towards your chest with your knees slightly bent. During this movement, your hips will rise upward. Continue the movement until your hips are over your shoulders in an inverted press position. Back to starting position. 

6.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Crunch From Hand
exercises.description:
1.When completing this exercise, ensure your abdominals are engaged and maintain neutral spine at a lengthen position.

2.When you’re in the crunch, shorten the distance between the bottom of your ribs and top of your hips.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a push up position by balancing your weight on your hands, arms and elbows straight.

4.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor. This will be your starting position.

5.Pull your legs towards your chest with your knees slightly bent. Your hips will rise upward. Continue the movement until your hips are over your shoulders in an inverted press position. Back to starting position. 

6.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Elbow Plank
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep a shorter distance between the bottom of your ribs and top of your hips. 

2.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a plank position by balancing your weight on your elbows. 

3.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor.

exercises.name: Suspension Full Plank From Hand
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your arms extended as well as maintain a shorter distance between the bottom of your ribs and top of your hips.

2.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a plank position by balancing your weight on your hands, arms and elbows straight. 

3.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor.

exercises.name: Suspension Hamstring Curl
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, use your glutes to use your body up off the mat.

2.Also, keep your abdominals on to ensure that you don’t overarch to your lower back.

3.Lie down on the mat, arms on the side, palms flat on the ground. 

4.With your feet suspended on the straps, lift your body up with your arms steady on the side. This will be your starting position.

5.Flex the knees, bringing your feet towards you. Then back to starting position.

6.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Hamstring Runner
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, lift from your glutes and keep your abdominals on. Don’t overarch through your lower back.

2.Lie down on the mat, arms on the side, palms flat on the ground. 

4.With your feet suspended on the straps, lift your body up with your arms steady on the side. This will be your starting position. 

5.Flex one knee, bringing your feet towards you. Then back to starting position. Do this alternately.

6.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension High Row
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, keep your core engaged and glutes on to maintain neutral spine.

2. When performing the row itself, you wanna pull back from the shoulder, keep the elbows at or above shoulder height as well as the hand coming above the shoulder.

3.If you need to make this exercise easier, walk your feet further away from the straps. If you wanna make it more challenging, walk your feet closer to the straps.

exercises.name: Suspension Hip Abduction
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, use your glutes to bridge up off the mat. Make sure your abdominals are set to not over arch to your lower back.

2. When you’re performing the lower body movement, move from your hips and keep your legs straight.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, Lie down the ground, palms flat on the floor. Then lift your hips off the mat. This will be your starting position.

4. Spread the legs as wide as you can while keeping your hips off the mat. Then back to starting position. 

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Hip Press
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, lift with your glutes. Ensure your abdominals are on preventing overarching through your lower back.

2.Lie down on the mat, arms on the side, palms flat on the ground. 

4.With your feet suspended on the straps, lift your body up with your arms steady on the side. 

5.Pushing your body up, lift your hips off the floor while keeping your back straight. 

6.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension I Delt Fly
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your abdominals and your glutes engaged to maintain neutral spine.

2.At the top of the fly, your arms will be directly overhead forming the letter I with your body.

3.Grasp the handles with both hands and your palms facing the floor. 

4.With your arms extended, lean back and adjust your relative position to change the degree of difficulty. 

5.With your body straight and your arms extended, you will be in the starting position. 

6.Pull yourself up keeping your elbows straight. Slowly return to the starting position.

7.To make this exercise easier, walk your feet away from the straps. To make it more challenging, walk your feet towards them.

exercises.name: Suspension Knee Tuck
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, try to shorten the distance between the bottom of your ribs and top of your hips as best you can. This will engage your abdominals the most.

2.With your feet suspended on the straps, arms extended, back and neck in a straight line, and core tight. This will be your starting position.

3. Pull your legs towards your chest with your knees slightly bent. Your hips will rise upward until your they are over your shoulders in an inverted press position. Then, back to starting position.

4.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Kneeling Roll Out
exercises.alternateName: TRX Kneeling Roll Out lat pull down
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure to maintain a short distance between the top of your hips and the bottom of your rib cage. This will ensure a safe back.

2.Keep your arms mostly straightened throughout the exercise.

3.With your back at the equipment, grab the straps with your arms in front of your body and your knees to the ground. This will be your starting position. 

4.Slowly descend your body while extending your arms until they’re above the head. Then back to starting position. 

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

6.To make this exercise easier, try shortening the straps. To make it more challenging, try a standing rollout.

exercises.name: Suspension Lateral Hip Drop
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your abdominals engaged. You’ll be working them as well as your hips.

2.Try to move primarily from the hips as you let them drop out and come back in line with your body.

3.Begin the exercise with both hands above your head while holding the straps. One foot in front of the other and your body is slightly towards the side.

4.Move your hips all the way to the side and using the straps to support your body keeping your hands above your head. Then back to starting position.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

6.When you complete one side, repeat on the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Long Torso Twist
exercises.alternateName: TRX lat and oblique, IT band stretch
exercises.description:
1.When performing this stretch, you may feel that in a number of different places. Ideally, you’ll be stretching out your hip, side of your abdominals and lats.

2.To set up, put your feet in line with one another.

3.Hold the handles with both hands then extend your body to the side while keeping your feet firmly on the ground. 

4.With your hips still bent, slowly twist your body so that you are completely facing forward. Hold for about 20 – 30 seconds. 

5.Then repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Suspension Low Back Stretch
exercises.description:
1.While performing the stretch, you’ll want to add traction to the back by hanging and sitting your hips back. But also letting your lower back or your lumber spine round out in a slight curvature. This will lengthen the lower back muscles.

2.Begin exercise with both hands holding the straps with arms straight, feet apart. 

3.Pull yourself backwards letting your lower back form a slight curvature then moving your feet forward to adjust the stretch.

4.You’ll wanna hold anywhere from 20 – 30 seconds long.

exercises.name: Suspension Low Back Stretch With Rotation
exercises.alternateName: TRX Low Back Stretch With Rotation, lat and shoulder stretch
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you’ll wanna sit your hips back adding traction to your spine and allowing the muscles on the low back inside of your body to stretch out.

2.To do so, you’ll gonna let your hip dropdown to the side and lengthen the side of your body as you rotate.

3.Begin exercise with both hands holding the straps with arms straight, feet apart. 

4.Pull yourself backwards letting your lower back form a slight curvature then moving your feet forward to adjust the stretch. This will be your starting position.

5.Then rotate your body to the side. You’ll wanna hold for about. 20 – 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Suspension Low Delt Fly
exercises.alternateName: TRX Low Delt reverse rear Fly
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your glutes on and core engaged to maintain neutral spine.

2.At the top of the fly, you’ll want your arms and your hands to be below your shoulders.

3.With your arms extended, lean back and adjust your relative position to change the degree of difficulty. 

4.With your body straight and your arms extended, you will be in the starting position. 

5.To make this exercise easier, move your feet away from the straps. To make it more challenging, move them towards.

exercises.name: Suspension Low Row
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna keep your core engaged and glutes on.  This will help maintain neutral spine.

2.When performing the row itself, you wanna pull your hands and elbows down towards the bottom of your rib cage.

3.With your arms extended, lean back and adjust your relative position to change the degree of difficulty.

4.To make this exercise more challenging, walk your feet towards the straps. If you wanna make it easier, walk your feet away.

exercises.name: Suspension Mid Row
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your core engaged and glutes on to maintain neutral spine.

2.When performing the row itself, you wanna pull your shoulders back, keeping the hand and elbow at or slightly below shoulder height.

3.With your arms extended, lean back and adjust your relative position to change the degree of difficulty.

4.To make this exercise easier, move your feet back, to make this exercise more challenging, walk your feet forward.

exercises.name: Suspension Mountain Climber
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure that you maintain neutral spine so keep your abdominals on.

2.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into starting position by balancing your weight on your hands, arms and elbows straight. 

3.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor. 

4.Alternately, bring the knee forward to the elbow on the same side of the body. 

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Neck Stretch
exercises.description:
1.When performing this stretch, any number of areas in our neck and shoulder might be stretched out, depending on what is tight and the angle of the head.

2.For this, we’ll aim to target the side of the neck. To do so, you’ll put your ear towards your shoulder.

3.Hold the straps with your one arm bent behind your back and gently tilt your head towards your shoulder. Hold for 20 – 30 seconds.

4.Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Suspension Oblique Atomic Push Up
exercises.description:
1.When completing this exercise, for the push up component, you’ll want your hand and elbow in line mid chest.

2.You’ll also wanna ensure that your abdominals are engaged so that you’re not arching your lower back. 

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, begin in the "up" position of a push-up: arms extended, back and neck in a straight line, and core tight. This will be your starting position. 

4.Descend into the bottom position of the push-up by flexing at the elbows until they reach a 90-degree position. Then back to starting position.

5.Then, pull your legs to the side with your knees slightly bent. During this movement, your hips will rise upward.

6.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Offset Stance Hip Hinge
exercises.alternateName: TRX Offset Stance Hip Hinge Hamstring stretch
exercises.description:
1.For this stretch, you’ll be stretching out your hamstring. In order to isolate it, you’ll want your knee straightened. And for your hips to tilt away.

2.Begin exercise with both hands holding the straps with arms straight, one leg extended forward, knees straight. 

3.Pull yourself backwards letting your lower back form a slight curvature.

4.Hold for 20 – 30 seconds and then repeat with the other side.

exercises.name: Suspension Overhead Back Extension
exercises.alternateName: TRX Overhead Back Extension swimmer pull
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna ensure that your arms are locked-out bicep to ears the entire time. Also, you’ll wanna keep your core tight.

2.You’ll be hinging from the hips so drive the exercise as you stand up from the lowered position by getting your glutes active.

3.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Overhead Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna maintain a safe and set up. What that means is you want your spine in a slightly arch position when you’re at the bottom of the squat.

2.Furthermore, you’ll want your arms in line with the side of your body which Is typically in line with the side of your head as well.

3.Start out by holding the handles with both arms raised all the way up. Your feet should be wider than shoulder width apart from each other. This will be your starting position.

4.Slowly lower your body by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the ground. Then back to starting position.

5.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Pendulum
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure a short distance between the bottom of your ribs and top of your hips. This will help prevent any lower back pain. 

2.As well, it’s an exercise that focuses on your abdominals. So be sure to move from the torso of your body.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into a push up position by balancing your weight on your hands - arms and elbows straight. This will be your starting position.

4.From your torso to your feet that’s suspended on the straps, move them to the side while keeping both arms steady to the ground. 

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Pike
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna ensure neutral spine throughout so keep your abdominals engaged.

2.As you go on to the pike position, use your hip flexors to push your hips into the air.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, get into starting position by balancing your weight on your hands, arms and elbows straight.

4.Keeping your body straight, lift your hips off the floor.

5.Pull your legs towards your chest with your knees straight. Push your hips all the way up then back to starting position. 

6.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Power Pull
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, set up the straps so that one handle goes through the other 1 to 2 times.

2.Check to make sure that the straps will hold. 

3.When you perform the movement, keep your abs engaged and glutes on maintaining neutral spine and a straight line throughout your body.

4.To make this exercise easier, move your feet back, to make this exercise more challenging, walk towards them.

5.Once you’re done performing the set on one hand, switch and do the other.
